parsed_review.summary: CP00-363 is a descriptor-only closeout pack (RP11.P09.M09.S01-RP11.P09.M10.S04, risk C, 12 units) that closes the RP11 Time Expense Disbursement descriptor program and hands off to RP12.P00.M00.S01/CP00-364. The pack-level posture is correctly NOT a runtime production claim: manifest and command-evidence both set production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, and the production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification flag only. All no-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are enforced in code (freezeCp363Result service.js:3024, CP363 no-write attestation registry.js:2099) and validated by tests and the contract validator; a Grep of packages/time-expense/src found zero fs/write/exec/spawn/network/db patterns, and the validator script only reads. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, validators.js:4833-4840), consistent with the supplied authority boundary (human final approval required; enterprise trust closed outside this receipt). Hermes patterns are used only for a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write) — no product runtime execution. Coverage (M09=8/M10=4), deliverable/phase/micro distributions, contract deep-equality with the descriptor, plan binding, README, and the registry/service/validators/contract/test/validator chain are all internally consistent, and the CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence hardening list is present. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this implemented_pending_review stage since they are generated from this review. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 cosmetic note on review-harness scope-file categorization. The pack-level production_ready (descriptor) claim is supported and the boundary constraints hold.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists the pack's own evidence directory as both implementation and unrelated
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-363/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md each appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). The pack's own evidence directory (which holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json for CP00-363) should not be categorized as 'unrelated'. The authoritative manifest.json worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:626-634) correctly excludes both, so this is a cosmetic inconsistency confined to the review-harness scope artifact. It does not affect scope enforcement, the descriptor-only/no-write boundaries, the production_ready posture, or the set of files actually in scope (the 8 active_pack_implementation_refs are correct and the unrelated workbook/docx/ldip files are correctly excluded).
findings.recommendation: In the scope-file generator, list the pack's own evidence directory and any pack-required untracked files under untracked_implementation_files only, and reserve unrelated_dirty_files_preserved for files outside the pack scope (as the manifest already does). Non-blocking; no code or closeout change required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking P3. Does not block pack closeout or goal closeout. Recorded for harness bookkeeping cleanup; no impact on production_ready claim or boundary compliance.
